reasoning: Acifluorfen is a diphenyl ether herbicide containing a nitro group and a fluorine substituent on the phenyl rings, with a carboxylic acid group. It fits as a nitrophenyl ether (a phenyl ether with nitro substitution), and also contains aryl halide and halophenol characteristics, but the diphenyl ether with nitro group makes nitrophenyl ethers the most specific direct parent.
